stem: An aquarium holds 40 gallons of water. If 1 US gallon = 3.785 liters, approximately how many liters of water does the aquarium hold? (Round to the nearest whole number.)
explanation:
To convert gallons to liters, multiply the number of gallons by the conversion factor.

$$40 \text{ gal} \times \frac{3.785 \text{ L}}{1 \text{ gal}} = 151.4 \text{ L}$$

Rounded to the nearest whole number: **151 liters**.

distractorRationale.A: Choice A (11) results from dividing instead of multiplying by the conversion factor — an inverted conversion: $40 \div 3.785 \approx 10.57 \approx 11$. The student uses the reciprocal of the correct factor, treating liters as the larger unit.
distractorRationale.B: Choice B (151) is the correct answer.
distractorRationale.C: Choice C (160) results from using the incorrect conversion factor 1 gallon = 4 liters instead of 3.785 liters: $40 \times 4 = 160$. A student who rounds the conversion factor up to 4 arrives at this answer.
distractorRationale.D: Choice D (182) results from using the UK (imperial) gallon conversion of 1 gallon \approx 4.546 liters instead of the US gallon value of 3.785 liters: $40 \times 4.546 \approx 181.8 \approx 182$. This is a metric/imperial system mix-up between US customary and UK imperial units.
